summary: Partially decompiled gm_801B0FF8 in gm_1A36.c, replacing the nonmatching placeholder with a typed static function that initializes card/snapshot/DVD/gallery state and maps current game mode values to fields in an argument substructure. The PR also tightened several library/menu prototypes and added minimal forward declarations and placeholder structs needed to express the function. No PR body or review comments were present in the slice.
what_changed: Implemented gm_801B0FF8 as a static void function taking struct gm_801B0FF8_arg0_t* instead of leaving it as an unmatched placeholder.
Removed the public gm_801B0FF8 UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS declaration from gm_1A36.h and added the typed static declaration to gm_1A36.static.h, indicating the symbol is treated as internal to gm_1A36.c.
Added local argument-shape structs gm_801B0FF8_arg0_t and gm_801B0FF8_arg0_x10_t, including a 0x10-byte pad before the x10 pointer and three s8 fields x0, x1, and x2.

gm_801B0FF8 now calls lb_8001C550, lb_8001D164(0), allocates snapshot buffers using HSD_MemAlloc with lbSnap_8001E210 and lbSnap_8001E204 sizes, passes them to lbSnap_8001E218, allocates four mnGallery_804A0B90 entries, initializes DVD/gallery systems, then uses gmMainLib_8015CC34 and current mode helpers to fill the output substructure.
Added a large switch over the mode value from gm_801A4320, with a special case remapping value 0x14 through gm_801737D8, to set temp_r31->x0 and temp_r31->x1 for many game/menu modes.
Changed helper prototypes from placeholders to concrete signatures: lb_8001C550(void), lb_8001D164(int), lbDvd_8001823C(void), and lbSnap_8001E218(void*, Unk80433380_48*).
Added a forward typedef for Unk80433380_48 in lb/forward.h and used it in lbsnap.h and lbsnap.c.
Expanded mngallery.h with address-commented declarations for mnGallery functions and an extern declaration for mnGallery_804A0B90[4].
Added struct mnGallery_804A0B90_t to mn/types.h as a 0x96000-byte placeholder with a STATIC_ASSERT for size.

Adjusted gm_1A36.static.h data declarations, changing several previously static data declarations such as gm_803DA788, gm_803DA920, gm_803DACA4, gm_803DBFD8, gm_803DD0F0, gm_803DD248, and gm_803DD2C0 to extern, while keeping gm_80479C30 as static.
smart_moves: Used narrowly scoped placeholder structs for gm_801B0FF8 arguments rather than leaving the function on UNK_PARAMS, enabling typed field accesses while avoiding unsupported claims about the full parent structure.
Converted dependency prototypes only as far as the new decompilation needed, reducing UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS usage for called functions without over-modeling their internals.
Preserved unknown structure contents with explicit padding and size assertions, especially mnGallery_804A0B90_t at 0x96000 bytes, which supports matching while documenting uncertainty.
Used ARRAY_SIZE(mnGallery_804A0B90) for the gallery allocation loop, avoiding a hard-coded loop bound despite the current array size being four.
Kept the complex mode-to-output mapping as a direct switch with explicit returns, which is likely matching-friendly for the original branch structure.
Introduced Unk80433380_48 as a forward declaration so lbSnap_8001E218 could gain a more accurate second-argument type without requiring the full snapshot struct definition in the header.
decomp_lessons: Partial matches often require nearby header cleanup: a function body may only become expressible after adding forward declarations, extern globals, exact helper prototypes, and minimal placeholder struct types.

When a decompiled static function was previously exposed in a public header as UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS, moving it to the static header with a typed signature can better reflect linkage and prevent bogus external API assumptions.
For argument structures discovered from offsets, a small local struct with named offset fields and padding is preferable to raw pointer arithmetic when enough layout is known.
Large unknown allocations can be represented by a named placeholder struct plus STATIC_ASSERT, preserving exact size while leaving semantic naming for future work.
Typing callee prototypes based on observed calls, such as void functions and known integer arguments, helps remove casts and makes matching code more idiomatic.
Evidence in this slice does not state whether gm_801B0FF8 fully matches; the PR title says partially match, so future readers should treat the function as a partial decompilation.
naming_conventions: New temporary unknown structs follow the project’s address-based placeholder style, e.g. gm_801B0FF8_arg0_t and gm_801B0FF8_arg0_x10_t.
Unknown fields are named by offset-like xN names, e.g. x0, x1, x2, and x10, with pad_0 used for unknown bytes.
Function declarations retain address comments such as /* 1B0FF8 */, /* 258940 */, and /* 4A0B90 */.
Unknown global-related types use address-derived names such as mnGallery_804A0B90_t and Unk80433380_48.
Known system prefixes are preserved: gm_ for game-mode, lbSnap_ for snapshot, lbDvd_ for DVD, mnGallery_ for menu gallery.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Used s8 fields in gm_801B0FF8_arg0_x10_t for byte writes to x0, x1, and x2, likely matching the original store-byte behavior.
Declared the loop counter as ssize_t and cast ARRAY_SIZE(mnGallery_804A0B90) to signed, suggesting attention to signed comparison/codegen details.
Placed repeated assignments in switch cases with immediate returns, matching a control-flow shape where each case writes output bytes and exits.
Called HSD_MemAlloc inline as arguments to lbSnap_8001E218 for the first snapshot buffer while storing the second allocation in a typed temporary, reflecting the observed dataflow.
Used exact allocation size via sizeof(struct mnGallery_804A0B90_t) and a STATIC_ASSERT to lock the placeholder to 0x96000 bytes.
review_feedback: No issue comments, review comments, or review records were present in the provided PR slice.
No reviewer rationale was available; conclusions are based only on the title, changed files, and diff excerpt.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that complete or modify gm_801B0FF8.
Search for semantic names replacing gm_801B0FF8_arg0_t or gm_801B0FF8_arg0_x10_t.
Search for later struct definitions or field names for mnGallery_804A0B90_t.
Check whether the mode switch constants in gm_801B0FF8 were later converted to enums.
Look for later changes to lbSnap_8001E218 or Unk80433380_48 to recover the real snapshot struct layout.
